title: Start with the project overview
description: README.md frames this repository as a React and Vite starter app. It explains the development model, hot module replacement, React plugin choices, and where the template expects linting guidance to grow.

title: Check the project commands and dependencies
description: package.json is the manifest that turns the README guidance into runnable commands. It defines the development, build, lint, and preview scripts, and it records the React, Vite, and ESLint packages the app depends on.

title: Find the browser entry shell
description: index.html is the first runtime file the browser loads. It provides the root DOM element for React and points Vite at /src/main.jsx as the module entry script.

title: Follow the React bootstrap
description: src/main.jsx creates the React root renderer, wraps the app in StrictMode, imports the global stylesheet, and mounts the App component into the DOM element declared by index.html.

title: Inspect the main UI module
description: src/App.jsx composes the starter interface. It imports local component styling and assets, then exports the component that renders the hero, counter, documentation links, and community links.

title: Zoom into the App component
description: The App function is the stateful center of the UI. It owns the counter state and wires the button click behavior that updates the rendered count.

title: Review component-level styling
description: src/App.css styles the view rendered by App.jsx. It handles the hero artwork, counter button, panels, responsive layout, and section separators using component-scoped CSS rules.

title: Review global styling
description: src/index.css sets the application-wide visual foundation. It defines theme variables, document defaults, typography, root layout, code styling, and dark-mode overrides.

title: Connect Vite to React tooling
description: vite.config.js configures Vite with the official React plugin. That plugin supports JSX transformation and React development behavior used by the source entry point.

title: Check code quality rules
description: eslint.config.js applies JavaScript and JSX linting for the project. It combines recommended JavaScript rules, React Hooks checks, Vite React Refresh guidance, and browser globals.

title: Understand the analysis boundary
description: .understandignore documents suggested ignore patterns for generated artifacts, editor files, logs, and test conventions. In this project, most entries are guidance rather than active exclusions.
